rationale: 2^64-1 is the largest unsigned magnitude that fits a native CBOR integer; per SPEC.md's bignum rule it MUST be a plain int (major type 0), never tag 2
smallest magnitude that must use tag 2 rather than a plain integer, per SPEC.md's bignum rule
one past the boundary; confirms the minimal 9-byte magnitude increments correctly with no leading zero byte
a wide 13-byte magnitude well beyond the boundary; the leading 0x10 byte plus trailing zero bytes confirm big-endian order and minimal-length (no leading zero) arbitrary-width encoding
16-byte all-ones magnitude; the widest positive bignum the u128-based reference adapters (Rust, C) accept — see the corpus follow-up on the arbitrary-width ceiling
-2^64 is the most-negative magnitude that fits a native CBOR integer (major type 1 encodes -1-arg, arg=2^64-1); per the bignum rule it MUST stay a plain int, never tag 3
smallest-magnitude value that must be a negative bignum, -(2^64+1); tag 3 stores magnitude-1 = 2^64, verifying CBOR's offset-by-one convention (encoded magnitude is |value|-1)
-(2^64+2); confirms the tag-3 offset-by-one stored magnitude increments correctly
wide negative bignum; the tag-3 stored magnitude 2^100-1 has a non-zero leading byte 0x0f that must be preserved (not stripped as a leading zero)
